text:
There was once a little old lady who lived atop a hill. Every morning when she got up, she would gaze out over the dewy grassy field and imagine tiny little creatures fluttering among the flowers.

One evening, she noticed a tiny shadowy figure dancing between the dandelions. It watched her closely when she sang birdsongs. She sang to the robin every morning because it would always come for her breakfast.

One day, while she was practicing her birdcall, the little thing peeped out from its flower bed. The old lady clapped her hands happily and sang its favorite tune. It ran back into its bed with laughter.

One week later, she sang her heart out during breakfast, just humming the cheerful tune. It peeked out once again. That time, it waved its tiny little hands.

She sang twice more that morning, just enjoying its cheerful presence.

Three weeks passed, and every morning, she would sing its favorite song, and it would dance out every
